message.content: Let's set up the database schema for the user management module

content.text: I'll help you set up the database schema. Let me first look at the existing project structure.

content: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);

message.content: We need tables for users, roles, and permissions with proper foreign keys

content.text: I'll create a comprehensive schema with users, roles, and permissions tables with proper relationships and constraints.

message.content: Good, now add created_at and updated_at timestamps to all tables

-- Updated with timestamps

message.content: Now let's add some seed data for the default admin role

content.text: I'll create a seed migration with the default admin role and basic permissions.

data.message: Starting research on RBAC best practices and common patterns for role-based access control systems.
Found that most modern RBAC systems use a hierarchical role model where roles can inherit permissions from parent roles.
Key finding: Google's Zanzibar paper recommends tuple-based authorization. AWS IAM uses policy-based model. Most web apps use simple RBAC.
Best practice: Separate authentication from authorization. Use middleware pattern for permission checks. Cache role lookups.
Recommendation for this project: Simple RBAC with user->role->permission chain is appropriate. No need for Zanzibar-level complexity.
Summary: Implemented schema follows standard RBAC pattern. Added suggestion for role hierarchy if needed later via parent_role_id self-reference.
Checking if migration files are compatible with common tools.
Migration format is standard SQL, compatible with most tools.

message.content: Actually, we should also add a soft delete column to users — deleted_at timestamp

content.text: Good call on soft deletes. I'll add a deleted_at column and update the queries to filter out soft-deleted records.

input.new_string:
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN deleted_at TIMESTAMP;
CREATE TABLE roles

message.content: Perfect. Let's run the tests to make sure everything works

content.text: Running the test suite to verify our schema changes.
